purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth stack where an error is returned if HCI_UNREGISTER flag has been set. This flag indicates that hci_unregister_dev has been called, which likely causes a use-after-free (UAF) after the timeout as the hdev will be freed.
The commit removes an unnecessary 'asm/termios.h' include in 'n_hdlc.c'. It appears to be a cleanup task and does not introduce any new features or functionalities.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' in store operations.

This commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb module of the Linux kernel by addressing two issues related to vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping. It also improves the code quality by updating the open vm_ops to allocate a new vma lock for the new vma, and updates the sharing code to make sure vma lock is indeed a condition for pmd sharing.
The commit adds two new supplies to the dt-bindings for the rt5682s codec. These are AVDD and MICVDD which were already used in sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call. The error handling path of the probe releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function.
The commit removes an unused field 'work' from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in net/mlx5e. This is a perfective change as it improves the code quality by removing unnecessary fields.
The commit is changing a struct member from unsigned to signed in order to avoid a warning from static checkers such as smatch. The change seems to be related to authentication and key management.
This commit is a configuration change that sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in a specific order. It does not introduce any new functionality or fix any bugs.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open coded check for CAN-FD mode.
The commit is about skipping some functional tests when a Real Time Clock (RTC) is not present. It seems to be related to the selftests module of the Linux kernel.
The commit is a refactoring that removes an unnecessary assignment to variable 'bytes'. The commit cleans up a clang scan build warning.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks. It does this by removing a dependency on RCU Tasks when building rcuscale.
The commit is removing a duplicated phrase in the file 'spte.h' which is located in the directory 'x86/mmu'. The change seems to be related to memory management.

The commit reverts a change in how dcn315 clk table is populated based on dcfclk. It appears to be related to preventing a bug where the pmfw might fill out the table with no read data when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds a new string for RZ/V2L SoC in dt-bindings: gpu: mali-bifrost. It documents the compatibility of this specific GPU model.
The commit renames a struct from 'pcie_port' to 'dw_pcie_rp'. It also updates the names of other entities in the codebase to maintain consistency.
The commit fixes a warning in the code related to formatting of an unsigned short integer.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture code related to branch range checking.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() function that calls another function directly.

The commit declares a symbol as static in an ARM64 architecture file. The symbol is 'cavium_erratum_23154_cpus' and it was not declared before. This change fixes sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.

The commit removes an interrupt flags cell from the MDSS nodes in dt-bindings display/msm. The purpose of this change is to correct a mistake where the MDSS interrupt provider was specified as a single cell, which is incorrect because the specifying interrupt flags on consumers is not applicable.
The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref. This is likely to be related to allowing drivers to change the array size.
The commit adds new PMICs and SoCs to a socinfo driver in the qcom subsystem of the Linux kernel. It seems to be adding support for more hardware platforms.
The commit recognizes all possible chip cuts from A to P in the rtl8xxxu driver.
The commit is about removing a software enable count from PRCMU clocks in the ux500 core. This change does not introduce any new features or improve code quality.
The commit is about refactoring code to improve readability and maintainability. It moves a macro used in XIP kernel implementation to be adjacent to the #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directive.
The commit enables QUP (Quick USB) and GPI DMA (Direct Memory Access) hardware in the sm6350-lena device for arm64 architecture. This is done to allow adding functioning I2C nodes later.
The commit is a bug-fixing commit that fixes a typo in comments and improves coding style.

The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function. The problem arises when failing to acquire a hardware semaphore, which is required for resource release. In this case, there's nothing to do but log an error and quit.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use more modern terminology for clocking.
The commit is removing a pointless hack that was used to check if a file was a swap file. The code is unnecessary as the IS_SWAPFILE macro is always defined.

The commit implements a GTP echo response mechanism for handling GTP messages. It involves creating an instance of GTP-U which would not respond to echo requests, and sends back a GTP_ECHO_RSP message with information element containing GTPIE_RECOVERY tag and restart counter value.
The commit is about replacing 'next' versions of cpumask functions with 'first' versions where appropriate. It also adds a new function called cpumask_first_zero().
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR from brcmfmac module. This is done in order to prevent the host driver from trying to get TIM information from firmware after roaming.
The commit adds support for a specific codec without an amplifier on a new platform. It also includes a board configuration file.
The commit is about adding error handling for zerocopy sendmsg requests that are not supported by the socket protocol. It fails these requests and does the same for zerocopy sendmsg.
The commit is about improving the situation by testing existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it. It's a perfective commit as it improves the quality of the code.
The commit is about adding a new macro definition in a header file for use by other parts of the kernel.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in drm/simpledrm module. The change is not functional but architectural.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding worker in MT8195 to ensure that three HWs run in parallel. The code changes are minimal and focused on adding an interface.
The commit message suggests that it is removing unnecessary print statements from the codebase.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD's display driver. It involves changes to how the driver handles Freesync state transitions.
The commit fixes a bug in the amdgpu driver related to Control Flow Integrity (CFI) violation. It updates the function pointer prototype of hqd_destroy() callback to match reality.
The commit corrects a typo in the 'resets' property name in dt-bindings for stm32. It also adds missing 'additionalProperties' to prevent future errors.
The commit is about silencing a GCC warning related to array bounds. It's about addressing an issue in the ath9k driver code.
The commit is changing a function call from pm_runtime_get_sync to pm_runtime_resume_and_get. This change seems to be made for simplifying the code.

The commit is about splitting out fixed file installation and removal into a separate module from the filetable code in io_uring. It also suggests that helpers should take a context rather than an io_kiocb to apply changes to the ring itself.
The commit updates DT bindings for two separate decoder blocks in the media subsystem of a device driver. It also adds support for power-domain control and fuses out the blocks independently.
The commit is removing a macro named IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON from the iwlwifi codebase.
